name: Big Monstera
_note: Repro for #109 — self-reinforcing β feedback loop. The first triple (May 14 watering → first post-water reading 3 days later) has no reading within 24h of watering, so β is seeded from a pure guess and comes out huge (~1.17/day). That inflated β then biases the max-peak selection in later triples toward later/lower readings, which confirm the high β. Net effect: β snowballs to ~0.85/day and the model predicts the plant much drier than it actually is. Fix: skip β-learning for long-gap triples, decouple peak-selection from the running β, and cap β. Timestamps anchored to fixed past dates for determinism. Notes from the wild (not modeled): May 17 5c watering leaked out the bottom; May 17/18 readings taken in a humid bathroom.
